title: Write setup with correct checkout-then-mode ordering
prompt: Write a `setup tags do` block inside `test/support/data_case.ex` that: (1) calls `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.checkout(MyApp.Repo)` FIRST, then (2) conditionally calls `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.mode(MyApp.Repo, {:shared, self()})` only when the test is NOT async (use `unless tags[:async]`). The order matters: checkout must establish ownership before mode/2 can share it. Include the full module header.
tier_rationale: Ordering matters but the solution is a canonical three-line pattern cited in Ecto docs.
research_citation: "For shared mode the checkout actually has to happen first. The checkout requests go through a GenServer which will have its state set with the owner's connection when changing the mode to shared" — Elixir Forum, joey_the_snake
